speaker: BLUNT
text: What is thy name that in the battle thus

speaker: BLUNT
text: Thou crossest me? What honor dost thou seek

speaker: BLUNT
text: Upon my head?

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Know then my name is Douglas,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: And I do haunt thee in the battle thus

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Because some tell me that thou art a king.

speaker: BLUNT
text: They tell thee true.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: The Lord of Stafford dear today hath bought

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Thy likeness, for instead of thee, King Harry,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: This sword hath ended him. So shall it thee,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Unless thou yield thee as my prisoner.

speaker: BLUNT
text: I was not born a yielder, thou proud Scot,

speaker: BLUNT
text: And thou shalt find a king that will revenge

speaker: BLUNT
text: Lord Stafford’s death.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O Douglas, hadst thou fought at Holmedon thus,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I never had triumphed upon a Scot.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: All’s done, all’s won; here breathless lies the King.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Where?

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Here.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This, Douglas? No, I know this face full well.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A gallant knight he was; his name was Blunt,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Semblably furnished like the King himself.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: A fool go with thy soul whither it goes!

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: A borrowed title hast thou bought too dear.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Why didst thou tell me that thou wert a king?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The King hath many marching in his coats.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Now, by my sword, I will kill all his coats.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: I’ll murder all his wardrobe, piece by piece,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Until I meet the King.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Up and away!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Our soldiers stand full fairly for the day.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Though I could ’scape shot-free at London,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I fear the shot here. Here’s no scoring but upon

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: the pate.—Soft, who are you? Sir Walter Blunt.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: There’s honor for you. Here’s no vanity. I am as hot

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: as molten lead, and as heavy too. God keep lead out

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: of me; I need no more weight than mine own

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: bowels. I have led my ragamuffins where they are

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: peppered. There’s not three of my hundred and fifty

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: left alive, and they are for the town’s end, to beg

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: during life. But who comes here?

speaker: PRINCE
text: What, stand’st thou idle here? Lend me thy sword.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Many a nobleman lies stark and stiff

speaker: PRINCE
text: Under the hoofs of vaunting enemies,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Whose deaths are yet unrevenged. I prithee

speaker: PRINCE
text: Lend me thy sword.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: O Hal, I prithee give me leave to breathe

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: awhile. Turk Gregory never did such deeds in arms

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: as I have done this day. I have paid Percy; I have

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: made him sure.

speaker: PRINCE
text: He is indeed, and living to kill thee.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I prithee, lend me thy sword.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Nay, before God, Hal, if Percy be alive, thou

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: gett’st not my sword; but take my pistol, if thou

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: wilt.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Give it me. What, is it in the case?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Ay, Hal, ’tis hot, ’tis hot. There’s that will

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: sack a city.

speaker: PRINCE
text: What, is it a time to jest and dally now?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Well, if Percy be alive, I’ll pierce him. If he do

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: come in my way, so; if he do not, if I come in his

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: willingly, let him make a carbonado of me. I like not

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: such grinning honor as Sir Walter hath. Give me

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: life, which, if I can save, so: if not, honor comes

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: unlooked for, and there’s an end.
